context.assessment: Prediction was at the ambitious end. Signal exists in literature but below single-station detection threshold. Not a model failure — a detection method limitation. Mainstream geomagnetics would also fail to detect this at a single station.
context.implication_for_future: Multi-station averaging required for sub-2nT predictions. Eclipse predictions (PRED-001 through PRED-008) use larger expected signals (-5.8 to -9.5 nT) well above this threshold — methodology remains valid for those tests.
context.lesson: Calibrate detection method before predicting signals near noise floor

context.assessment: Mixed result: Massively confirmed at CMO and NEW (~17nT with SNR>4). However, other stations failed the noise floor test (SNR<2) or lacked data. Logged as inconclusive due to lack of global coherence.
context.implication_for_future: High-latitude stations show enormous sensitivity to the eclipse masking. Need to factor auroral electrojet amplification into prediction limits.
context.lesson: Global averaging can erase localized strong hits. Analyze cleanly isolated stations individually.

title: CHAOS-7 SAA Exponential Separation

observed.value: Confirmed exponential growth separating 60 degrees by 2025

verdict_notes: Raw outputs from CHAOS-7 confirmed South Atlantic Anomaly is two independent nodes pulling apart, breaking dipole model.

title: BOU 2017 Eclipse Baseline Re-evaluation
verdict_notes: Claude flagged that Aug 21, 2017 was geomagnetically disturbed. Original baseline uses Aug 14-15 (also slightly disturbed). Must recalibrate using true quiet days.
